title: Numbers: Worship and Departure
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: worship and departure and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: worship and departure and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What guided Israel's movement through the wilderness — when to stop and when to march? Answer: the cloud over the tabernacle: when it lifted, Israel marched; when it settled, Israel camped.
segments.correct_answer: the cloud over the tabernacle — when it lifted Israel marched, when it settled they camped
acceptable_variants: a cloud by day and fire by night over the tabernacle
when the cloud lifted they moved, when it rested they stopped
the pillar of cloud and fire directed all movement

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The visible manifestation of God's presence guiding Israel through the wilderness; by day a cloud and by night a fire, it directed every march and rest
Two silver trumpets in Numbers 10 used to signal Israel's movements and assemblies; they established order for the march and called leaders to meet Moses
